title: Wellhead Telemetry Data Gap Monitoring
app: Edge Hub / RTU feeds via HEC
spl:
index=ot sourcetype="wellhead:telemetry"
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen by well_id
| eval gap_sec=now()-last_seen
| where gap_sec > 900
| eval gap_min=round(gap_sec/60,1)
| sort - gap_sec
| table well_id, last_seen, gap_min
description: Missing wellhead measurements indicate communication failures. Quick detection ensures SCADA and field teams can restore data continuity.
value: Service and risk leaders use signals from Wellhead Telemetry Data Gap Monitoring to move crews, budget, and customer or regulator communications while the situation is still controllable, so the organization steers from live data rather than a post-incident report.
implementation: Include expected interval on each event or use 3x scan rate as threshold. Verify against planned shutdowns via a maintenance lookup. **Domain context:** Wellhead SCADA gaps can mask unsafe operating conditions or production loss—align thresholds with RTU poll schedule (minutes vs seconds). **Splunk:** `now()-last_seen` in scheduled searches uses search time; use `latest(_time)` from a summarization for fleet-wide freshness.
visualization: Table (wells by gap), Single value (stale well count), Timeline (last seen).
knownFalsePositives: Gap clusters after solar-power swaps on radios, plunger-lift transients, or comms work on the lease road that the pumper and SCADA support annotated.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Edge Hub / RTU feeds via HEC.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ot` `sourcetype="wellhead:telemetry"` fields `well_id`, `expected_interval_sec`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Include expected interval on each event or use 3x scan rate as threshold. Verify against planned shutdowns via a maintenance lookup. **Domain context:** Wellhead SCADA gaps can mask unsafe operating conditions or production loss—align thresholds with RTU poll schedule (minutes vs seconds). **Splunk:** `now()-last_seen` in scheduled searches uses search time; use `latest(_time)` from a summarization for fleet-wide freshness.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot sourcetype="wellhead:telemetry"
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen by well_id
| eval gap_sec=now()-last_seen
| where gap_sec > 900
| eval gap_min=round(gap_sec/60,1)
| sort - gap_sec
| table well_id, last_seen, gap_min
```

Understanding this SPL

**Wellhead Telemetry Data Gap Monitoring** — Missing wellhead measurements indicate communication failures. Quick detection ensures SCADA and field teams can restore data continuity.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ot` `sourcetype="wellhead:telemetry"` fields `well_id`, `expected_interval_sec`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Edge Hub / RTU feeds via HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot; **sourcetype**: wellhead:telemetry. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot, sourcetype="wellhead:telemetry". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by well_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **gap_sec** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where gap_sec > 900` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **gap_min** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
• Pipeline stage (see **Wellhead Telemetry Data Gap Monitoring**): table well_id, last_seen, gap_min


Step 3 — Validate
Compare trendlines with the PI or site historian and field operations tickets for the same asset and window. Align maintenance and flaring events with what site leadership logged.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (wells by gap), Single value (stale well count), Timeline (last seen).
grandmaExplanation: We watch wellhead telemetry for gaps. We help you catch dead sensors before production reports go wrong.
